0.1_purpose.summary: Cross-package machine-readable rollup for the Canonical Delegation & Enablement Matrix V2 release.
0.1_purpose.role: Provides a consolidated navigation index from responsibility and obligation IDs to delegated entities, governed objects, enablement families, surface types, contracts, gaps, doctrines, taxonomy updates, and downstream ontology seed candidates.
0.1_purpose.non_semantic_rule: This rollup index must not introduce new canonical semantics. It may only aggregate, normalize, cross-reference, and validate semantics already defined in packages 01 through 05.
0.1_purpose.governing_mapping_law: Responsibility / Obligation -> Primary Delegated Entity -> Supporting Entities -> Governed Objects -> Enablement Family -> Surface Type -> Trace
0.1_purpose.governing_trace_law: External Relation -> Contract -> Responsibility -> Obligation -> Delegation -> Enablement -> Trace Coverage -> Ontology Candidate

3.0_family_rollups.summary: Covers typed ingress, validating versus hot-path separation, validation placement, mandatory timestamp stabilization, and Log Level Envelope realization.
Covers policy bundle intake, Policy Object identity, assignment mediation, component mechanism hosting, and safe-point replacement.
Covers metadata directive intake, schema/directive-governed injection, metadata authority path preservation, and metadata-to-envelope composition.
Covers operational directive intake, pause/drain/scale/activate realization, writable degradation visibility, and policy-conforming queue order.
Covers registry readiness, envelope handoff gating, Dispatcher boundary, receiving admission, delivery-cycle flags, and lifecycle non-ownership after handoff.
Covers Log Level Envelope construction, admission, identity preservation, movement, lifecycle transfer, and distinction between occupancy binding and lifecycle ownership.
Covers non-authority exclusion, surface separation, Dispatcher Boundary Law, template contract doctrine, Policy Object doctrine, and Queue-as-Container doctrine.

Covers queue-bundle orchestration, single queue-container moderation, slots, occupancy binding, slot lifecycle, waiting lists, queue-container identifiers, requalification, dispatcher feedback reports, and queue-container terminology preservation.

5.0_enablement_rollups.anti_collapse_rule: Assignment surface must not collapse into content ingress, mechanism execution, metadata injection, or queue runtime behavior.
Policy Object governs mechanism semantics; component hosts mechanism locally; Metadata Injector is excluded.
Log Level Envelope remains runtime carrier; raw content, queue-container identity, and occupancy binding must not replace envelope identity.
Queue equals Container; Queue Runtime Layer is classification-only; waiting lists store Queue Container Identifiers.
Registry / Delivery Port is boundary contract surface; Dispatcher remains final assembler-side handoff actor.
Delivery-Cycle Flag Set and Dispatcher Feedback Report must remain separately queryable artifacts.
Boundary, exclusion, and authority edges must remain explicit and must not be erased by managerial convenience.
Typed/template contracts are canonical contract laws, not runtime COM interfaces.
Metadata Injector is schema/directive-governed; Timestamp Stabilizer is mandatory and mature; neither requires a placeholder timing slot.

6.0_gap_rollups.anti_regression_rule: Registry / Delivery Port may expose the contract surface, but Dispatcher remains the outbound actor and assembler lifecycle authority ends after valid handoff.
PolicyAssigner assigns; Policy Object governs semantics; host components realize mechanisms locally; Metadata Injector remains schema/directive-governed and is not a policy host.
BCO-013 Delivery-Cycle Flag Set is compact state; BCO-014 Dispatcher Feedback Report is detailed diagnostic/reporting artifact; they must not collapse.
Timestamp Stabilizer is a mature mandatory component; no placeholder timing-slot semantics may remain where stabilized timestamp is required.
Queue Bundle Agent orchestrates bundles; Single Queue Moderator moderates one queue-container; round managers own round waiting discipline; Queue Runtime Layer remains classification-only.
Queue equals Container; waiting lists store Queue Container Identifiers; Envelope Slot Occupancy is binding/state only; Log Level Envelope lifecycle transfers only at valid handoff.
Envelope Assembler is the client-side composed contract object; Validator participates only in validating ingress; hot-path must not silently reintroduce validation.

9.0_anti_collapse_guards.canonical_rule: Metadata Injector is schema/directive-governed and must use metadata/timestamp preparation semantics, not policy mechanism hosting.

9.0_anti_collapse_guards.blocked_collapse: Queue Runtime Layer as primary delegated entity
9.0_anti_collapse_guards.canonical_rule: Queue Runtime Layer is classification-only; actual responsibility carriers are Queue Bundle Agent, Single Queue Moderator, and round managers.

9.0_anti_collapse_guards.blocked_collapse: Delivery-Cycle Flag Set as Dispatcher Feedback Report or vice versa
9.0_anti_collapse_guards.canonical_rule: BCO-013 is compact delivery-cycle state; BCO-014 is detailed diagnostic/reporting artifact.

9.0_anti_collapse_guards.blocked_collapse: Separate runtime container above Queue Container
9.0_anti_collapse_guards.canonical_rule: Queue equals Container; no separate runtime container may be introduced above Queue Container.

9.0_anti_collapse_guards.blocked_collapse: Waiting lists storing Log Level Envelopes
9.0_anti_collapse_guards.canonical_rule: Writing and delivery waiting lists store Queue Container Identifiers, not Log Level Envelopes.

9.0_anti_collapse_guards.blocked_collapse: Envelope Slot Occupancy as Log Level Envelope lifecycle owner
9.0_anti_collapse_guards.canonical_rule: Envelope Slot Occupancy is binding/state only; lifecycle authority transfers only at valid dispatcher-registry handoff.

9.0_anti_collapse_guards.guard_name: Registry Port Does Not Replace Dispatcher
9.0_anti_collapse_guards.blocked_collapse: Registry / Delivery Port as final handoff actor replacing Dispatcher
9.0_anti_collapse_guards.canonical_rule: Dispatcher remains the final assembler-side outbound actor; Registry / Delivery Port is the registry-facing contract surface.

9.0_anti_collapse_guards.blocked_collapse: Typed/template contract as runtime COM interface
9.0_anti_collapse_guards.canonical_rule: Contracts remain static/canonical typed/template laws and must not become runtime COM-interface semantics.

11.3_non_semantic_rollup_rule.rule: No new canonical responsibilities, obligations, entities, governed objects, enablements, surfaces, contracts, gaps, or doctrines are introduced by this file.

13.0_final_canonical_statement.statement: This rollup index is the canonical cross-package navigation artifact for the Canonical Delegation & Enablement Matrix V2 release. It consolidates responsibility, obligation, delegation, enablement, contract, gap, taxonomy, and anti-collapse traceability without introducing new architecture.
non_canonical_if: a responsibility appears here that is not present in packages 01 through 05
an obligation appears here without aligned responsibility
a new delegated entity appears here without upstream registration
a governed object is promoted into an actor without upstream taxonomy update
Metadata Injector is treated as policy-consuming
Queue Runtime Layer is treated as a primary delegated entity
Delivery-Cycle Flag Set and Dispatcher Feedback Report are conflated
Log Level Envelope is treated as Queue Container Identifier
Envelope Slot Occupancy is treated as lifecycle ownership
typed/template contracts are converted into runtime COM interfaces
